message:
fix(net,grpc): wake evicted waker instead of silently discarding it

When the 32-entry waker vec cap was added (f2fde139), evicted waiters
were silently discarded via remove(0). If more than 32 concurrent
acceptors existed, the oldest one would never be woken — a permanent
lost wakeup.

Fixed by calling wake() on the evicted waker before removing it, so
the evicted task gets a chance to re-poll and re-register. Applied
to all 4 sites: tcp/listener, tcp/virtual_tcp, unix/listener,
grpc/client.

Also fixes GF2 column_pivot docstring: said 'lowest set bit' but
implementation returns highest_bit() (correct for persistent
homology per standard column reduction algorithm).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
